NXT-65553.summary: INV - Internal Vendors - Contracts - Distribution Settings
NXT-65553.ac:
h3. Design Requirements

* Expand the Contract section for internal vendors to include new settings for ordering and distribution
* Include all attributes when adding a new contract, but split the edit functionality for existing contracts
** Contract Info
** Distribution Preferences
* Make UI changes that are applicable to both Internal and External vendors
** Change Contract Number to Contract Name
** Relabel the Min/Max Purchase Amount to ‘Purchase Amount Limits’ and put Minimum and Maximum on a single row
** Remove the Comments field
* Note: fix the strange drop shadow and bordering present on this grid (compare to other tabs)
* Use the ‘Back to Contracts’ button as both a Back and Cancel button when sub-navigating from the Contracts list
** ex: display ‘Back to Contracts’ if no data is changed (dirty), but change the button to ‘Cancel’ if user modifies data

----

h3. Settings

* Modify the setting for displaying available quantity to sites [https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-66208|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-66208|smart-link] 
** Change toggle to a dropdown
*** label: ‘Display Available Quantity to Sites’
*** 3 options: Projected QOH (default), Actual QOH, Do Not Display QOH
** Provide an info icon with a clickable (not mouseover) fly-out explainer: “This setting determines which value for the distribution site’s Available Quantity is displayed to sites when ordering. Projected QOH will consider future transactions, while Actual QOH will strictly reference the perpetual value at the time. You may also choose to hide the Quantity Available value from ordering sites.”
* Provide a new toggle setting for whether or not sites can order more than the distribution site QOH
** label: ‘Allow Orders to Exceed Available Quantity'
** default to ‘Allow’ (enabled)
** if ‘Do not display QOH’ is selected for the ‘Display Available Quantity to Sites’ setting in the row above, forcibly set the toggle to ‘Allow’ and disable the toggle switch
** Provide an info icon with a clickable explainer: “This setting determines whether or not sites can order quantities greater than the distribution site’s Available Quantity. The value of the ‘Display Available Quantity to Sites’ setting will be considered for determining which QOH value is used to limit orders. Please note, this setting cannot be disabled if ‘Do Not Display QOH’ is selected for the prior setting.”
* Introduce a new setting for ‘Source of Substitutions’ [https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-66209|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-66209|smart-link] 
** Dropdown w/ 2 options: Site Perpetual, Contract Items
** Default: Site Perpetual
** currently this is hardcoded to consider only contract items
** Provide an info icon with a clickable explainer: “When this option is set to Site Perpetual, any item in the distribution site’s inventory may be added to distributions for item substitutions or additions. When this option is set to Contract Items, only items on the vendor contract may be added to distributions.”
* Provide a toggle setting for whether or not items can be added without being a substitution
** label: 'Allow Non-Substitution Added Items'
** if this is set to ‘Do not allow' then all added items must be substituted
** Provide an info icon with a clickable explainer: “This setting determines whether items can be manually added to distributions. When this option is set to Allow, the distribution site can freely add items to a distribution without substituting for another item. When this option is set to Do Not Allow, the distribution site can only add items as part of a substitution.”
* Provide a setting for how orders will be received when the distribution for a day is already Assigned [https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-66210|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-66210|smart-link] 
** this would override the existing Approve/Reject framework
** label: Allow Orders After Assignment
** 3 options:
*** Prompt Each Time (default) (current behavior)
*** Auto-Create New Distribution
*** Disallow orders after Assignment
** Provide an info icon with a clickable explainer: 
*** “This setting determines what should happen when orders are placed on a date for which a Distribution is already in Assignment status. 
Prompt Each Time will tentatively add orders to the existing distribution and allow the distribution site to decide whether to include them or move them to a new distribution.
Auto-Create New Distribution will accept orders after assignment, but they will automatically go to a new distribution transaction.
Disallow Orders After Assignment will completely prevent sites from ordering once that date is in Assignment status.”
NXT-65553.desc:
As a Distribution Manager I want settings on the internal contract so I can customize certain distribution details for my district’s operations.

h3. 

h3. Internal Vendors - Contract List 

* Change Contract Number to Contract Name
* Settings icon added to Action column

NXT-66738.summary: INV - (Internal) Vendor - Ordering Conditions - Hide Unassigned and Default groups in the grid
NXT-66738.ac:
* Use the Manage Views framework on the Ordering Conditions grid to hide certain columns
* Hide the Unassigned delivery group by default
* Hide the Default delivery group if it has no associated sites
* Display the Unassigned group as the last delivery group in the Manage Views slide-out
NXT-66738.desc:
we do not need to treat this delivery group like the other groups for the purpose of displaying in this grid. Since these sites cannot order, we don’t want to display the Unassigned group like it’s a regular delivery group.

!image-20250919-200253.png|width=494%,alt="image-20250919-200253.png"!

That being said, rather than removing it as a column entirely, I think a better solution would be to hide the column by default using our Manage Views tool, and to always display as the last delivery group in the column order.

!image-20250919-200407.png|width=479%,alt="image-20250919-200407.png"!

We could also apply similar logic to the Default group, and hide the column when there are no sites in the group. This behavior would match what we have done on the Shipping Schedule regarding the changes to Default and Unassigned.

NXT-68734.summary: Insights - Insights Dashboard Page - Inventory Value KPI (Backend)
NXT-68734.ac:
* Using the Inventory Valuation Report calculations
** Inventory value:
*** For today, this week, this month, year to date - it will be calculated as of today and 11.59pm
*** yesterday - as of yesterday 11.59pm
*** last week - as of last day in the last calendar week at 11.59pm
*** last month- as of last day in the last month at 11.59pm
*** prior year, any numbered previous years -  end of the year inventory value taking the time at 11.59pm
*** For Custom date range: its going to be calculated as of the selected "to date" date at 11.59pm
* Benchmark/Expected value/Inventory total goal:
** when all schools selected (or multi site selection if its allowed) and for the selected Academic year: sum of  site benchmark values for all schools 
** when a specific school selected for the selected academic year: display the value set for the selected school and year.
** Today, yesterday, last week, this week, this month, last month, year to date, -takes the current academic year benchmark
** prior year: one year prior to current academic year
** numbered years in the past -takes the value from the specific year
** Custom date range: Display the benchmark value set for the academic year for the "to date" (ideally from and to dates will be in the same academic year , if not we will take the academic year for the to date selected in the custom date range. )
NXT-68734.desc:
As a district inventory manager, I want to view the total Inventory value at any given time and for all or selected school, so that I can monitor stock levels and make decisions on purchasing or redistribution. Helps maintain optimal inventory levels to avoid overstocking or stock outs.

NXT-68735.summary: Insights - Dashboard Page - Physical Inventory Discrepancy KPI (Backend)
NXT-68735.ac:
* Step#1: For the selected time period dropdown option  , find all physical inventories with Reconciled status and counting completed date time with in the start and end dates of the selected time period dropdown option.
* Step#2 : sum the discrepancy amounts of all physical inventories resulted in above step#1 and from all selected sites. 
* Show the percentage of total inventory by calculating the total inventory value from all selected sites ( Refer to  [+NXT-68734+|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-68734]  for details on how to Calculate the total inventory value )
* SPs/APIs provided should support for displaying the site breakdown for all periods and  for a selected storage category, item category and item type  or for a specific item etc..
NXT-68735.desc:
As a school district director, I want to view the total discrepancy amount across all schools so that I can investigate potential causes such as spoilage, theft, or misreporting.

NXT-68736.summary: Insights - Insights Dashboard Page - Inventory Turnover Rate  (Backend)
NXT-68736.ac:
* Using Data from the Inventory Usage report
** Inventory turnover rate is calculated as : (Beginning Inventory + Purchases During Period) – Ending Inventory /(Beginning Inventory + Ending Inventory) ÷ 2 (Note:  All values are dollar amounts not inventory Quantity)
** Purchases are calculated as: Receipts +transfer in+ add to inventory - (transfer out+ returned amount)
** When all schools and more than one school is selected : apply the above formula for all schools or selected sites and all items. 
** One school selected: apply the formula for the selected school items. 
** Inventory turnover rate when less than a month (today, yesterday, last week, this week) is selected : calculated for the current month, if current month has just started and its value is zero then it fall back to year to date.
** For any other time period selection, it will be calculated using the formula for the selected date range and school(s)
** SPs/APIs should be provided for breakdown of site(s ),time period, storage category(s) ,Item category(s),item type(s),Item(s) selection 
** SPs/APIs should cover the inventory turnover rate for both Quantity based and Dollar based options
NXT-68736.desc:
As an inventory manager , I want to track the inventory turnover rate for each period, so that I can understand how quickly inventory is used and identify inefficiencies in stock usage.

NXT-68832.summary: INV - Vendor Contracts - Multiple Observations : We can save the contract without receiving an overlap error message, even though it overlaps.
NXT-68832.desc:
INV - Vendor Contracts - Multiple Observations :

# We can save the contract without receiving an overlap error message, even though it overlaps.
# Even after the main contract became inactive, the seasonal contract remains active.
# Even after the main contract becomes inactive, adding menu items to the seasonal contract syncs those items with the inactive main contract (06/01/2025 to 12/03/2025), despite the days not aligning with the seasonal contract.

Steps to reproduce:

# Select an active main contract for e.g. from (06/01/2025 to 12/31/2025),
# Create a seasonal contract from (12/21/2025 to 12/31/2025), - This status will displays as Seasonal which is as expected,
# Now change the main contract days from (06/01/2025 to 12/03/2025), - This contract will change the status from active stage to inactive stage,
# Now create an another contract from (12/02/2025 to 12/20/2025) - in this stage user should get an error popup telling contract days overlapped but it simple saved the contract,
# Observe that the seasonal contract created with the first main contract still displays the status as seasonal without linking to that main contract. If we add a menu item to this seasonal contract, it syncs with the inactive main contract, even though the dates do not match.

NXT-69149.summary: INV - PO Observations - INV - Internal Vendors - Contracts - Seasonal Contracts
NXT-69149.desc:
Kindly find the below comments which has been added while reviewing the [https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-64328|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-64328|smart-link] story, by PO :

The date validations are perfectly sufficient for handling this. We should never be clearing user data on basic UI triggers like this.

*Edit:*

I can’t seem to recall exactly when we discussed this, but Lakshmi provided the supposed logic of this behavior.

If your intention is to use the UI to force users to enter valid data, that goal is not being met. Instead, this is just making the UI a more confusing experience.

You modify the date picker to not allow me to pick months beyond the main contract, but I can still type whatever date I want, so this modification to the date picker is not accomplishing anything.

Likewise, even after forcibly clearing the date fields and modifying the date picker, you still had to show an error message. If we have an error message, what is the point of all this aggressive UI behavior? Just let the user enter what they want and use the validations.

We need to keep things simple. Users are not going to understand why you are arbitrarily clearing their data or why they cannot click somewhere they can usually click. 




--> Kindly note that images are not getting added in this NXT, hence we need to refer the story comments while working on the same
